notes: Compiled manually via cross-referenced web search (Discogs, Wikipedia, AllMusic, jazzmusicarchives, AllAboutJazz, Encyclopedia.com, flatint blog, RateYourMusic, Album of the Year, Last.fm, Apple Music, Bandcamp). NOT verified against a full Discogs API pull. Treat as a baseline to diff against, not ground truth. status field: 'confirmed' = appears consistently across 2+ independent sources; 'tentative' = appears in only 1 source or with conflicting year/title info; 'gap' = known missing region, not yet researched.
sort_key_convention: year field uses recording_year if known and confidently sourced, else release_year. Both are given separately where known.

albums.title: Duke Ellington Presents the Dollar Brand Trio
albums.label: Reprise

albums.title: Anatomy of a South African Village
albums.label: Reprise

albums.title: The Dream
albums.label: Reprise
albums.note: Verified 2026-06-16: recorded live 30 Jan 1965, Jazzhus Montmartre, Copenhagen; original release 1965 (Reprise). Reissued multiple times (vinyl reissues 1979 and 1980, CD reissue 1991 as 'Dollar Brand Trio'). See order 29 -- the previously-flagged '1979 duplicate' is one of these reissues, not a separate work.

albums.title: This Is Dollar Brand
albums.label: Black Lion
albums.note: Recorded 1965, released 1973 -- recording/release gap.

albums.title: Sangoma
albums.label: Sackville
albums.note: Recorded Toronto, 18 Feb 1973. Shares session material with African Portraits.

albums.title: African Portraits
albums.label: Sackville
albums.note: Same Toronto session as Sangoma.

albums.title: Fats, Duke & The Monk
albums.note: Possibly overlaps with Sangoma material ('Fats, Duke and the Monk' is also a track/suite title on Sangoma per Wikipedia) -- needs disambiguation, may be a duplicate not a separate album.

albums.title: Ancient Africa
albums.label: JAPO/ECM
albums.note: Recorded June 1972 at Jazzhus Montmartre, Copenhagen; released 1974.

albums.title: Good News from Africa
albums.label: Cameo/Enja
albums.note: Duo with Johnny Dyani. Recorded 10 Dec 1973, Ludwigsburg, Germany.

albums.title: Memories
albums.label: Philips
albums.note: Verified 2026-06-16: real, distinct album, not a duplicate of 'Duke's Memories' (a different, later compilation). Recorded 12 Dec 1973, Tonstudio Bauer, Ludwigsburg, Germany; released 1974 on Philips. Part of the 1973-74 German-session cluster alongside Good News from Africa.

albums.title: Underground in Africa
albums.note: Listed 'with Oswiete' per AllMusic narrative.

albums.title: Mannenberg (Is Where It's Happening)
albums.label: Celluloid (France)
albums.note: Title track became an unofficial anthem for Black South Africans.

albums.title: African Breeze
albums.label: Enja / East Wind
albums.note: Verified 2026-06-16: real, distinct live album. Recorded live 21 Feb 1974; released 1974 (catalog appears under both East Wind and Enja). Resolves as one of the genuine titles in AllMusic's '~9 releases in 1974' cluster.

albums.title: The Children of Africa
albums.note: Listed as Dollar Brand.

albums.title: Blues for a Hip King
albums.note: Verified 2026-06-16: this is the original 1976 studio/session album. See order 40 for the 1989 release of the same title, which is a compilation, not a duplicate original -- resolved, not a conflict.

albums.title: Cape Town Fringe
albums.note: Listed as Dollar Brand.

albums.title: The Journey
albums.note: Some sources list 1978; AllMusic narrative says 1977. Features Don Cherry, Hamiet Bluiett.

albums.title: Duet
albums.note: Archie Shepp & Dollar Brand.

albums.title: Autobiography
albums.label: Plainisphare (LP) / Elephant (CD reissue 2001)
albums.note: Recorded 18 June 1978, Switzerland. Two-disc live LP.

albums.title: The Dream (1979 reissue)
albums.note: RESOLVED 2026-06-16: this is a 1979 vinyl reissue of order 3's 1965 recording, not a separate album. Kept as a row for Discogs cross-reference completeness; excluded from the primary chronological list via the 'relation' field.

albums.title: Dollar Brand at Montreux / Live at Montreux
albums.label: Enja

albums.title: Ekaya
albums.note: Major work; Ekaya becomes name of his long-running band.

albums.title: Water from an Ancient Well
albums.label: Tiptoe
albums.note: Some sources list 1986. Highly regarded; title reused on 2024 live tracks ('3').

albums.title: Mindif
albums.note: Title later performed live again in 2023, appears on '3' (2024).

albums.title: Voice of Africa
albums.note: Possible compilation, not yet disambiguated from studio output.

albums.title: Blues for a Hip King (1989 compilation, The African Recordings)
albums.note: RESOLVED 2026-06-16: this is the 4th volume in the 'complete African recordings' compilation series, reusing the title of order 21's 1976 original album plus tracks from other sessions. Not a duplicate/conflicting original release. Kept as a row for completeness; excluded from the primary chronological list via the 'relation' field.

relation.compiles_from_note: Also draws from Underground in Africa (1974), Black Lightning (1976), and Dollar Brand Plays Sphere Jazz (1962).

albums.title: Desert Flowers
albums.label: Enja (ENJ 70112)
albums.note: CORRECTED 2026-06-16: original Encyclopedia.com-derived 1982 year was wrong. Confirmed release date is July 1992 on Enja (ENJ 70112); AllMusic's 1991/1992 citation was the accurate one. Reordered accordingly (was incorrectly placed next to the 1982 cluster).

albums.title: Knysna Blue
albums.note: Source rendered as 'xnysna Blue', likely an OCR/scrape error for 'Knysna Blue' (a town in South Africa).

albums.title: African Suite (for Trio and String Orchestra)
albums.note: CONFLICT: jazzmusicarchives says 1999, Encyclopedia.com narrative says 1998. First classical work.

albums.title: Sotho Blue
albums.label: Sunnyside
albums.note: Credited to Abdullah Ibrahim & Ekaya.

albums.title: Mukashi (Once Upon a Time)
albums.label: Sunnyside
albums.note: RESOLVED 2026-06-16: single album, not two parallel regional releases. Recorded fall 2012-spring 2013, Hansahaus Studios, Bonn, Germany; mixed summer 2013; released 2014 on Sunnyside (US). The earlier 'JAPO' label note was an error -- JAPO (an ECM-affiliated label used for 1969's African Piano) is unrelated to this release; Discogs' 2013/2014 listings reflect promo vs. general-release dating, not separate regional editions.

albums.title: The Song Is My Story
albums.label: Sunnyside
albums.note: Solo piano and saxophone.

albums.title: Universal Silence
albums.label: Lepo Glasbo
albums.note: IMPORTANT TEST CASE: live document with Don Cherry & Carlos Ward, recorded 1972, released 47 years later in 2019. Strongest example in this discography of the recording/release-date gap.

albums.title: Dream Time
albums.note: Distinct from 'The Dream' (orders 3 and 29) -- confirm not a mistaken duplicate.

albums.title: The Balance
albums.label: Gearbox (UK)
albums.note: With nonet version of Ekaya. Last.fm shows 14 Aug 2019 / 27 Jun 2019 listed separately -- possible region/format date conflict.

albums.title: Solotude
albums.label: Gearbox
albums.note: IMPORTANT TEST CASE: solo concert recorded on his 86th birthday during COVID lockdown, no audience, at Hirzinger Hall, East Germany. Released 2021 per RateYourMusic/Last.fm, 2022 per AllMusic narrative -- needs final disambiguation but the recording context is consistent across sources.

albums.label: Gearbox
albums.note: Recorded Barbican Hall, London, 15 July 2023 (Vol. 1 direct-to-tape, no audience; Vol. 2 live concert same evening). Released in advance of his 90th birthday. Trio: Cleave Guyton Jr. (flute/piccolo), Noah Jackson (bass/cello).

open_questions: No full Discogs API/title-by-title pull was performed -- verified 2026-06-16 that the Discogs artist page (id 248560) totals match this dataset's premise (70 releases: 55 albums + 13 compilations), but a complete title-by-title cross-check of all 70 against this list has still not been done. Recommended as the first automated step when the pipeline is built (Discogs has a real API; this was checked manually via search/fetch this session).

2024-2026 output: checked 2026-06-16 (the day after his death, reported 15 June 2026 in Prien am Chiemsee, Germany). No releases found after '3' (2024). No posthumous/archival release has surfaced yet, which is expected given the timing -- worth rechecking in a few months.
